initial_posts.narrative: As a master's student in AI at Hanyang University, I see the long-term impact of BK21's sustained investment in graduate talent development. Over a decade, the program has built a pipeline of highly skilled researchers who now lead labs and collaborate internationally. The 400 graduate slots and 30 postdocs per year have produced a steady stream of alumni who have entered academia and industry, boosting South Korea's innovation capacity. The dissemination of outstanding group achievements through the BK21 webzine and forums has spread best practices in research integrity, collaboration, and interdisciplinarity across institutions. This systemic change in research culture is reflected in the rising number of high-impact publications. Based on my experience, the program's focus on international conference support and collaboration incentives has increased the rate of top-cited publications. I estimate South Korea's rank in global S&T talent competitiveness will improve to around 8th, and high-impact publications per 1000 researchers will be about 12.5, considering the active cohort of 400 graduate students and 30 postdocs in the 2021-2022 window. Other participants might underestimate the cumulative effect of a decade of investment, but my perspective as a beneficiary shows the tangible improvements in research quality and collaboration.
evidence: The BK21 program's sustained investment in 400 graduate slots and 30 postdocs per year over a decade has created a critical mass of highly trained researchers who now drive innovation in academia and industry, improving national competitiveness.

forward_pass.phase_summary: The group discussed how BK21's decade-long investment in graduate students and postdocs has built a pipeline of skilled researchers, boosting South Korea's innovation capacity and research culture through dissemination of best practices.
